roles.core: central bot, D1 authority, payment callbacks, report outbox, safe update controller and Durable Object live usage engine
roles.edge: reseller bots, customer miniapps and imported-subscription lifecycle with normalized reseller growth settings
roles.processor: advanced schedules, auto-renewal, service health, reseller tiers, backups, campaigns and background reports

features: moves 13 BluePanel 3.3 growth and automation settings from reseller_bots into reseller_growth_settings
prevents any new ALTER TABLE operation for the 13 settings that caused sqlite_altertab_reseller_bots column-limit failures
self-heals databases left with one or more partially added 3.3 columns and copies those values into the normalized table
keeps the original reseller_bots table at the stable 3.2 column layout for fresh installations
hydrates growth settings transparently in Core, Edge and Processor so previous feature behavior remains available
updates toggles, low-balance thresholds, reseller tiers, auto-renew and service-health jobs to use the normalized table
splits snapshot restore between legacy reseller settings and normalized growth settings
retains emergency fail-open Telegram webhook behavior and all previous 3.2/3.3 fixes

deployment.emergency_recovery: deploy core/worker.js once to the existing Core Worker because the currently running recovery Core cannot complete a ZIP rollout while schema bootstrap is failing; after the bot returns, upload this full ZIP through the central bot
deployment.automatic_provisioning: supported from the central bot ZIP uploader after Core recovery

limitations: the first recovery step still requires deploying the Core file once because the currently running Core is already trapped in schema recovery mode
production Cloudflare, Telegram and PasarGuard calls were not executed without account credentials
legacy partial growth columns already added to reseller_bots are left in place but are no longer read or written
